exercises.name: Dumbbell Step-Up to Overhead Press
exercises.alternateName: Single side Step-Up to Shoulder press
exercises.description: Using dumbbells, step up and stand tall on the platform, then press the weights overhead.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Step-Up to Shoulder Press
exercises.description: Using dumbbells, step up and stand tall on the platform, then press the weights overhead.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Straight Leg Deadlift
exercises.description:
1)  When performing this deadlift, ensure that your back is safe by keeping your chest up and a slightly arched in your back as you lower the weight. 

2) For the straight leg deadlift, don’t bend your knees.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Sumo Deadlift
exercises.description:
Stand tall holding one dumbbell with both hands in front of your thighs, feet wider than shoulder-width apart, toes pointing slightly outward. Hinge at your hips and bend your knees slightly to lower the dumbbell toward the floor, keeping your back straight and chest up. 

Engage your glutes and hamstrings to push through your heels and return to standing, bringing the dumbbell back to the starting position.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Sumo PlieÌ Squat
exercises.description: Dumbbell Sumo PlieÌ Squat

exercises.name: Dumbbell Sumo Squat
exercises.description:
Stand tall holding a dumbbell with both hands in front of your chest, feet wider than shoulder-width apart, toes pointing slightly outward. Keep your chest up and core engaged. 

Lower your body by bending your knees and pushing your hips back, keeping the dumbbell close to your chest, until your thighs are parallel to the floor (or as low as comfortable). Push through your heels to return to standing.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Swing to Squat
exercises.description: Using dumbbells, swing the weight to shoulder height with a hip hinge, then squat down and stand back up.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Thruster
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, the dumbbells will rest up by your shoulders. 

2) As you get into the squat position, keep your head and chest up, sit back into your heels. 

3) Pop out of your legs and extend your arms, locking biceps to ear. 

4) Return the weight to your shoulders. 

5) Repeat.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Tricep Press
exercises.description:
Lie flat on a bench holding dumbbells close together, palms facing each other. 

Lower the dumbbells to your chest, then press them back up, targeting the triceps and inner chest.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Turkish Get-Up
exercises.description:
Lie on your back holding a dumbbell in one hand, arm fully extended toward the ceiling. Bend the knee on the same side as the dumbbell, keeping the opposite leg straight on the floor. 

Press through your foot and roll onto your opposite elbow, then rise onto your hand while keeping the dumbbell overhead. 

Push through your foot to lift your hips into a bridge, sweep your straight leg underneath you, and come into a kneeling lunge position. From here, stand up fully while keeping the dumbbell overhead. Reverse the movement to return to the starting position.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Upright Row
exercises.description:
1.Stand and hold dumbbells in front of your body with arms hanging and thumbs facing inwards. 

2.Pull dumbbells straight up till just below your chin and your elbows out. 

3.Pause at the top.

4.Return to starting position.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Walking Lunge
exercises.description:
1) When performing your lunges, ensure that your upper body stays over top of your back knee. 

2) Front knee doesn’t reach forward over top of your front toes.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Walking Lunge Curl to Shoulder Press
exercises.description: Holding dumbbells at your sides, step forward into a lunge, curl the weights to your shoulders, then press them overhead as you stand up. Step forward with the other leg and repeat, alternating as you walk.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Walking Lunge into Bicep Curl
exercises.description: Using dumbbells, take alternating walking lunges, then curl the weights to your shoulders.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Walking Lunge with Bicep Curl
exercises.description: Step forward into a walking lunge while curling the dumbbells to your shoulders at the same time. Continue stepping forward, alternating legs.

exercises.name: Dynamic Frog Stretch
exercises.alternateName: Frog Stretch Balasana Variant adductor stretch
exercises.description:
1. When performing this mobility exercise, you'll want to slowly and progressively move your knees further apart.

2. Wait until you're in a comfortable distance and then move it further out as you need a deeper stretch.

3. Position yourself on your hands and knees on the ground. Knees spread apart. This will be your starting position.

4. Move your glutes backward. Then back to starting position.

exercises.name: Dynamic Hamstring Stretch
exercises.description:
1. Lie on your back, with one foot flat on the mat and the other leg fully extended with toes pulled towards the knee.

2. Slowly raise your fully extended leg up until you feel a gentle stretch in the back of your thigh, then return to the start position. 

3. Repeat for desired repetitions, then do the same on the other side.

exercises.name: Dynamic Hip Flexor Stretch
exercises.description:
1. When you're doing this mobility exercise, you'll want to ease into and then back out of it and repeat slowly as to not over stretched of have the muscles contract.

2. Kneel on a mat and bring your right knee up so the bottom of your foot is on the floor and extend your left leg out behind you so the top of your foot is on the floor.

3. Shift your weight forward until you feel a stretch in your hip. Hold for 15 seconds.

4. Once you're done one side, repeat it on the other.

exercises.name: Dynamic Hip Opening Flow
exercises.description:
1. Step forward into a lunge position and place your hands on the floor in front of you. 

2. Reach the hand nearest to the front foot up towards the ceiling while rotating your torso towards your front foot. 

3. Reach and rotate back down towards the floor, then reach and rotate all the way back up again. 

4. Return to a plank position, and push yourself up into downward dog - with your hips up and hands overhead. 

5. Step forward into a lunge on the other side and repeat the same sequence.

exercises.name: Dynamic Pigeon Stretch
exercises.description:
1. When performing this mobility exercise, you'll want to ease into and out of the pigeon pose. 

2. Get down on the floor, roll your right leg underneath you, stretch your left leg back. Pausing briefly. 

3. Use your elbows to support the upper body. 

4. Lift your body up by straightening both arms. Then back to starting position.

5. When you've completed one side, do the other.

exercises.name: Dynamic Rear Foot Elevated Hip Flexor Stretch
exercises.description:
1. When performing this exercise, feel free to hold on to something for balance.

2. You'll also want to keep your glutes activated when you're on the active stretch part to make sure that your hip flexors are anchored and actually getting a good stretch.

3. Kneel on a mat and bring your right knee up so the bottom of your foot is on the floor and extend your left leg out behind you so the top of your foot is on the floor.

4. From behind you, grab your foot and pull it closer to your glutes while moving your body forward.

5. When you've completed one side, repeat it on the other.

exercises.name: Dynamic Side Lunge Stretch
exercises.description:
1. For the inside of your thigh, alternate from left to right holding roughly 3 seconds per stretch before moving on to the other side.

2. With the knees slightly bent, feet shoulder-width apart, and the head and chest bent forward. This will be your starting position.

3. Take a slow, lateral step to the right. Keep your toes pointed forward and stay low. Extend the left knee, driving your weight to the right, flexing the knee and hip into a side lunge. 

4. Pause at the bottom of the motion, and then extend through the working leg to return to a standing position, transitioning into a lunge to the opposite side.

exercises.name: Dynamic Wall Angel Facing Wall
exercises.description: Dynamic Wall Angel Facing Wall

exercises.name: Eccentric Push Up
exercises.description:
Start in a high plank position. Lower your body slowly to the ground over 3-5 seconds, maintaining a straight line. 

Push back up or reset to the top position to repeat.

exercises.name: Elbow Plank
exercises.description:
1.Lie face down on a mat with elbows resting on the floor next to your chest, palms facing forward or in a fist position, feet together.

2.Push your body off the floor in a pushup position with your body resting on elbows or hands. 

3.Contract the abs and keep the body in a straight line from head to toes. Hold for 30-60 seconds, then lower body returning to starting position.

4.Repeat.

exercises.name: Elbow Plank Knee Drops
exercises.description:
In an elbow plank, drop one knee to the ground without shifting your hips. 

Alternate sides. Maintain core engagement.

exercises.name: Elbow Side Plank
exercises.description:
1) When performing the side plank, ensure that your body is in a straight line and your elbow underneath your shoulder. 

2) Squeeze your glutes to keep your hips forward in line with your body. 

3) Once in position and you have a nice straight line down your body, hold.

exercises.name: Elevated Bar Push-up
exercises.description:
1. Start with your hands on the bar at shoulder-width.

2. Assume a plank position, and lower your chest towards the bar while keeping a stiff body.

3. Press yourself back up to the start position and repeat.

4. To make the exercise harder, lower the bar towards the floor.

exercises.name: Elevated Glute Bridge to Chest Press
exercises.description:
Lie on your back with your shoulders resting on a bench and your feet flat on the floor, holding a dumbbell in each hand at chest level. 

Press through your heels to lift your hips into a glute bridge. At the top of the bridge, press the dumbbells overhead. 

Lower your hips and dumbbells back to the starting position and repeat.

exercises.name: Elevated Pike Push-Up
exercises.description:
Place your feet on a bench or elevated platform and your hands on the floor, forming a pike position with your hips raised. 

Lower your head toward the floor by bending your elbows, then push back up to the starting position. 

Repeat continuously, keeping your core engaged and hips high throughout the movement.

exercises.name: Elevated Plank Row
exercises.description: Get into a plank position with one hand gripping a dumbbell placed on a bench or elevated platform and the other hand flat on the bench for support. Keep your body straight from head to heels. Row the dumbbell toward your ribcage while keeping your hips and core stable, then lower it back down.

exercises.name: Elevated Single Leg Hip Thrust to Single Arm Chest Press
exercises.description:
Lie on your back with your shoulders resting on a bench or an elevated platform. 

Place one foot flat on the floor and hold a dumbbell in the hand opposite that foot. As you drive your hips upward into a single-leg hip thrust, lift the other leg and bend it into a knee-drive position. 

At the same time, press the dumbbell overhead. Lower your hips and dumbbell back to the starting position and repeat.

exercises.name: Elevated Single-Leg Glute Bridge to Chest Fly
exercises.description:
Lie on your back with your shoulders resting on a bench or an elevated platform. Place one foot flat on the floor and hold a dumbbell in each hand at chest level. Extend the other leg slightly so it’s in a knee-drive-like position and keep it in this position throughout the movement.

Drive your hips upward through the foot on the floor into a single-leg hip thrust. At the top, perform a chest fly by opening both arms wide and bringing them back together over your chest. Lower your hips and dumbbells back to the starting position

exercises.name: Elevated Squat
exercises.description: Elevated Squat

exercises.name: Enter The Belt Squat (My BOS Review)
exercises.description: Enter The Belt Squat (My BOS Review)
